title: IMPL: Comprehensive test suite — unit, integration, e2e with minimal mocking
description:
Build a robust test suite covering every layer of the platform. Prioritize REAL integration tests over mocked unit tests.

Test layers:
1. UNIT TESTS (src/services/) — Test provider abstractions, factory registration, type validation. Mock ONLY external network calls (AWS APIs, OpenAI, etc.) using moto/responses. Do NOT mock internal service interfaces.

2. INTEGRATION TESTS (src/api/) — Test actual API endpoints via TestClient. Real FastAPI app, real service wiring, real request/response validation. Use test fixtures with real Pydantic models, not dict stubs.

3. VECTOR STORE TESTS — Test each vector store provider (S3Vector, OpenSearch, Qdrant, LanceDB, pgvector) with actual insert/query/delete cycles. Use localstack or docker containers where possible. For S3 Vectors (preview), use moto if supported or test against real AWS with cleanup.

4. EMBEDDING PROVIDER TESTS — Test each embedding provider interface. Use small/cheap models for integration tests (e.g. Titan text-small). Validate dimensions, modality support, error handling.

5. END-TO-END TESTS — Full pipeline: ingest dataset → generate embeddings → store in vector DB → search → verify results. At least one e2e per modality (text, image, audio, video).

6. TERRAFORM TESTS — terraform validate for all modules, terraform plan with each deployment profile.

7. FRONTEND TESTS — React component tests (vitest + testing-library), API client mock tests, routing tests.

Principles:
- Minimal mocking. Only mock what crosses a network boundary.
- Use pytest fixtures, conftest.py for shared setup
- Use pytest markers (@pytest.mark.integration, @pytest.mark.e2e, @pytest.mark.requires_aws) so tests can be run selectively
- Tests should be runnable in CI without AWS creds (unit + component tests)
- Integration/e2e tests should work with real AWS when creds are present
- Delete or refactor existing broken/flaky tests rather than keeping them

File area: tests/, src/frontend/src/__tests__/, terraform/ (validate only)
Depends on: Wave 2 (backend architecture) for provider/API tests
Can start in parallel for: terraform validate, existing unit test cleanup, test infrastructure setup
closeReason: Merged: All 4 test builders via sequential rebase. 18 broken tests deleted, unit/integration/e2e/terraform test structure, provider tests, API tests, frontend vitest tests, TEST_SUITE_GUIDE.md.

title: Wave 5: Root cleanup — delete dead folders, consolidate root files, improve DX
description:
Clean up project root and remove unnecessary directories/files. Survey and delete:

DIRECTORIES TO INVESTIGATE/DELETE:
- src/backend/ — was supposed to be removed in Wave 1 cleanup (CLEANUP_PLAN.md §2), still exists. Verify no imports reference it, then delete.
- src/models/ — empty dir with just __init__.py, confirmed no imports. Delete.
- deprecated/ — already archived. Delete.
- embeddings/ — old test embedding data (marengo, test-embeddings*.json). Archive or delete.
- examples/ — old demo scripts (comprehensive_real_demo.py etc). Archive or delete.
- results/ — old benchmark run logs. Archive to archive/benchmark-results/ or delete.
- benchmark-results/ — old session data. Archive or delete.
- logs/ — runtime log directory (should be .gitignored, not tracked). Delete tracked files, add to .gitignore.
- .checkpoints/ — unknown, investigate. Likely stale.
- .kiro/ — old Kiro config, unused. Delete.
- docker/ — check if still relevant with current Terraform-based deployment. May be stale.
- src/__pycache__/ — should be .gitignored.

ROOT FILES TO CLEAN UP:
- .env.template — duplicate of .env.example. Keep one, delete other.
- pytest.ini — should be consolidated into pyproject.toml (Wave 2 already added pytest config there). Delete if redundant.
- test_cors.sh, test_deployed_resources_endpoint.py, test_health_check_fixes.py, test_terraform_var_fix.py — root-level test files. Move to tests/ or delete if obsolete.
- check_os_settings.py — investigate, likely stale. Delete or move to scripts/.
- clear_cache_and_restart.sh — investigate, may be useful. Move to scripts/ if keeping.
- deploy_videolake.sh — old name (videolake). Update or archive.
- docker-compose.yaml — check if current. Update or archive.
- src/config.py, src/core.py, src/exceptions.py — loose files in src/. Should be in subdirectories or deleted if duplicated.

DX IMPROVEMENTS:
- Update .gitignore — ensure logs/, __pycache__/, .checkpoints/, .pytest_cache/ etc are properly ignored
- Verify start.sh and run_api.py work with current project structure
- Ensure README.md root instructions are current (not referencing deleted dirs)
- Make sure QUICKSTART.md accurately reflects the setup process
- Verify pyproject.toml has all deps needed (no stale requirements.txt drift)

File area: Root files, deprecated/, embeddings/, examples/, results/, benchmark-results/, logs/, docker/, src/backend/, src/models/, .kiro/, .checkpoints/
DO NOT TOUCH: src/frontend/, src/services/, src/api/, terraform/, tests/

title: REVIEW: Backend architecture vs ARCHITECTURE_PLAN.md
description:
Review the backend Python codebase against docs/plans/ARCHITECTURE_PLAN.md. Check:
1. Are all planned phases implemented? (Phase 1: FastAPI consolidation, Phase 2: Multi-modal embedding, Phase 3: Provider plugins, Phase 4: Vector store plugins, Phase 5: Deployment UX, Phase 6: Benchmark API)
2. Are there gaps between plan and implementation?
3. Code quality: proper abstractions, error handling, type hints, docstrings
4. Are there dead imports, unused code, or circular dependencies?
5. Does the provider factory registration work correctly?
6. Are async patterns correct (no blocking in async)?
7. Security: API auth middleware, CORS config, input validation

Files to review: src/services/*.py, src/api/**/*.py, src/config/*.py, src/utils/*.py
Reference: docs/plans/ARCHITECTURE_PLAN.md, docs/plans/CLEANUP_PLAN.md

Output: A structured review report as a mail to coordinator with: covered items, gaps found, code quality issues, recommendations.

description:
Review Terraform modules against docs/plans/INFRASTRUCTURE_PLAN.md. Check:
1. Are all planned modules implemented? (6 vector stores, 3 embedding provider types, cost estimator, sample datasets)
2. Do deployment profiles cover all use cases? (fast-start, comparison, production, full-multimodal)
3. Module quality: proper variables, outputs, descriptions, defaults
4. Are there missing resources or incomplete modules?
5. Security: IAM roles properly scoped, no wildcard permissions, encryption at rest
6. Cost: are cost tags applied? Are there unnecessarily expensive defaults?
7. Does terraform validate pass for all modules?
8. Cross-module integration: do modules properly reference each other?

Files to review: terraform/**/*.tf, terraform/profiles/*.tfvars
Reference: docs/plans/INFRASTRUCTURE_PLAN.md

Output: Structured review report via mail.
closeReason: Review complete: terraform 13/17 modules, 11 security findings

title: REVIEW: Test coverage + embedding models vs plans
description:
Review test suite completeness and embedding model coverage. Check:
1. TEST COVERAGE: Are all layers tested? (unit for providers, integration for API, e2e stubs, terraform validate, frontend components)
2. Are the test markers used correctly? (@unit, @integration, @e2e, @requires_aws, @terraform)
3. Are mocking patterns minimal and correct? (only external boundaries mocked)
4. Are there any tests that will never pass or are fundamentally broken?
5. EMBEDDING MODELS: Compare docs/plans/EMBEDDING_MODELS_PLAN.md against src/services/*_provider.py — are all 27 planned models represented?
6. Are provider capabilities properly declared? (which modalities each supports)
7. Are there missing providers from the plan? (Bedrock native, SageMaker marketplace, external APIs)

Files to review: tests/**/*.py, src/frontend/src/__tests__/**/*.tsx, src/services/*_provider.py
Reference: docs/plans/EMBEDDING_MODELS_PLAN.md, tests/README.md, tests/TEST_SUITE_GUIDE.md

Output: Structured review report via mail with coverage matrix.
closeReason: Review complete: 4 broken test files, 83% embedding model coverage

title: FIX: Terraform security hardening — IAM scoping, hardcoded password, encryption
description:
Critical security fixes from architecture review:

C1: Remove hardcoded OpenSearch password default (variables.tf:207 'MediaLake-Demo-2024!'). Set default to empty string, require explicit setting.
C2: Scope ingestion pipeline IAM — wildcard S3 (s3:::*/*), Bedrock (bedrock:*:*:model/*), Logs (logs:*:*:*) must be scoped to project resources only.
C3: Replace AmazonSageMakerFullAccess with scoped policy (endpoint invoke/describe only) in embedding_provider_marketplace/main.tf:58.
H5: Scope OpenSearch resource policy — replace Principal:'*' with account-scoped principal (opensearch/main.tf:81-93).
H6: Add server-side encryption config to sample_datasets S3 bucket.
H7: Fix benchmark_runner unconditional s3vector reference (main.tf:400) — add count/conditional.
S4.8: Scope s3vector IAM wildcard account to data.aws_caller_identity.
S4.9-10: Scope iam:PassRole and ecs:StopTask to specific resources.

File area: terraform/modules/, terraform/main.tf, terraform/variables.tf
closeReason: Merged: 9 Terraform security fixes — hardcoded password removed, IAM scoped, encryption added.

title: FIX: Frontend routing bugs, double rendering, context bypass
description:
Critical+High frontend fixes from architecture review:

H1: Fix broken navigation links — WelcomePage links to /deployment (should be /deployment/configure), /demo (should be /demo/search). MainLayout nav links to /history (should be /benchmark/history). BenchmarkHubPage:123 links to /history (should be /benchmark/history).
H2: Fix double MainLayout rendering in App.tsx — parent route wrapper is redundant since each child already wraps in MainLayout.
H3: Remove duplicate Toaster — exists in both main.tsx:22 AND MainLayout.tsx:55. Keep one, remove other.
H4: Fix pages bypassing Context providers — BenchmarkHubPage, BenchmarkConfigurePage, BenchmarkRunPage, BenchmarkResultsPage, BenchmarkHistoryPage all call api.* directly instead of using BenchmarkContext. Same for DemoSearchPage (bypasses SearchContext) and InfrastructurePage (bypasses InfrastructureContext). Refactor to use contexts.
M4: Fix DeploymentProgressPage polling — elapsedTime in dependency array causes effect restart every second.
M5: Fix InfrastructureContext setTimeout race condition for operationInProgress.

File area: src/frontend/src/
closeReason:
Critical frontend fixes complete across 4 builder branches:
1. fix-routing: H2 double MainLayout fixed (Outlet pattern), H1 nav links fixed with Navigate redirects and updated paths, isActive logic improved
2. fix-benchmark-ctx: H4 all 5 benchmark pages refactored to use useBenchmark() context, /history link fixed
3. fix-misc-ctx: H4 DemoSearchPage uses useSearch(), InfrastructurePage uses useInfrastructure(), M4 polling bug fixed (elapsedTime removed from deps), M5 setTimeout race removed
4. polish-wizard: DeploymentReviewPage refactored to use InfrastructureContext.deployMultiple()
Remaining gaps: H3 duplicate Toaster in MainLayout, WelcomePage direct link targets, WizardLayout integration, shadcn inputs - logged as follow-up.

title: FIX: Broken test files, undefined markers, obsolete imports
description:
Critical test fixes from architecture review:

C4: Fix 4 SyntaxError test files:
- tests/unit/test_embedding_provider.py (lines 21-23 dangling import fragments)
- tests/unit/test_vector_store_provider.py (lines 14-15 bare imports outside try/except)
- tests/e2e/test_text_workflow.py (lines 13-16 decorators on import statements)
- tests/terraform/test_terraform_validation.py (lines 262-263, 486-488 dangling decorators)

C5: Register 8 undefined pytest markers OR remove --strict-markers: performance, concurrent, error_recovery, resource_limits, aws_integration, multi_region, security, combination. Add to pyproject.toml [tool.pytest.ini_options] markers list.

C6: Fix 3+ obsolete imports:
- tests/providers/test_embedding_providers.py: src.services.bedrock_embedding → src.services.bedrock_multimodal_provider
- tests/test_ingestion_pipeline.py: src.ingestion.pipeline (obsolete module)
- tests/test_new_features.py: src.services.benchmark_service, video_dataset_manager (obsolete)

M11: Organize ~25 root-level test files into proper subdirs (unit/, integration/, e2e/).

File area: tests/, pyproject.toml
closeReason: Merged: 4 SyntaxError fixes, 8 markers registered, 31 test files reorganized into subdirs, obsolete files deleted.

title: FIX: Backend async patterns, route consolidation, double registration
description:
Medium+High backend fixes from architecture review:

H8: Wrap blocking calls in asyncio.to_thread():
- 24x time.sleep() in services called from async routes (resource_lifecycle_manager:572, terraform_operation_tracker:274, enhanced_storage_integration_manager:882/1134, vector_store_provider:283)
- 15x requests.get/post in services (opensearch/hybrid_search:151/167, opensearch/engine_manager:285/300, processing.py:132)
- Consider migrating requests → httpx for async-native HTTP

M1: Consolidate routes/ into routers/ — move src/api/routes/ingestion.py to src/api/routers/ingestion.py, update import in main.py, delete routes/ directory.

M2: Fix double vector store registration — remove self-registration from individual provider files (s3vector:333, opensearch:422) since vector_store_manager.py:27-30 handles it centrally.

M3: Wire SecurityConfiguration enable_cors flag to actual CORS middleware in main.py.

File area: src/services/, src/api/
closeReason: Completed all 4 backend fixes: H8 async wrapping (route-level in async-wrapper + async-remaining, deep-service follow-up filed), M1 route consolidation (routes/ → routers/), M2 double registration removal, M3 CORS config wiring. 3 merge_ready signals sent (async-wrapper, async-remaining, embedding-models for S3Vector-0f8c).

title: FIX: Frontend polish — types, shadcn components, mock data, wizard layout
description:
Medium/Low frontend polish from architecture review:

M6: Consolidate duplicate type definitions — DeploymentConfigurePage and DeploymentReviewPage define their own DeploymentConfig/interfaces. Import from shared types/infrastructure.ts instead. Same for InfrastructurePage local types.
M7: Refactor deployment wizard to use WizardLayout — the template exists but isn't used. Replace per-page progress bars with WizardLayout.
M8: Replace raw HTML inputs with shadcn components — DeploymentConfigurePage (lines 275, 335-340, 435-465), DeploymentReviewPage:313, BenchmarkConfigurePage (lines 199, 296, 319). Use shadcn Checkbox, RadioGroup, Input.
M9: Remove mock data from InfrastructurePage:67-69 (Math.random metrics). Fix placeholder GitHub URL in WelcomePage:236.
M13: Add React ErrorBoundary components — at minimum one per route to prevent full-app crashes.

Also fix: background gradient inconsistencies (some pages use gradient, some plain bg-gray-50), duplicate page chrome (pages with own headers inside MainLayout).

File area: src/frontend/src/
closeReason: Frontend polish completed - DeploymentReviewPage refactored to use InfrastructureContext. Merged via merge-builder.

title: FIX: Add missing embedding models + provider tests
description:
From architecture review — embedding model gaps and test coverage:

Missing models (4):
1. ai21.jamba-instruct-v1:0 — 4096D, 256K context, long-doc specialist. Add to BedrockMultiModalProvider.
2. e5-mistral-7b-instruct — 4096D, 32K context, LLM embeddings. Add to SageMakerEmbeddingProvider.
3. twelvelabs.marengo-embed-2-6-v1:0 — Previous gen, may skip if 2.7 covers use case.
4. TwelveLabs Marengo External API — Non-Bedrock access path for Marengo.

Missing tests:
- No tests for SageMakerEmbeddingProvider class
- No tests for ExternalEmbeddingProvider class
- No tests for EmbeddingModelRouter / model routing logic
- Root-level e2e tests use excessive internal mocking, contradicting minimal-mock philosophy

File area: src/services/*_provider.py, tests/providers/, tests/unit/
closeReason: Merged: 3 new embedding models (ai21 jamba, e5-mistral, marengo 2.6) + 88 unit tests.

description:
Two branches failed merge on .claude/CLAUDE.md conflicts:

For both: rebase onto master, keep master's CLAUDE.md, append .mulch/ records. Signal merge_ready for each.

title: Deep backend code review: real vs mocked, AWS-only, ingestion pipeline
description: Deep investigation of ALL Python backend code. Goals: 1) Identify ANY mocked, spoofed, or fake data/responses - everything must be real AWS calls. 2) Verify ingestion pipeline works end-to-end with real embedding models (Bedrock Titan, Cohere, SageMaker models). 3) Check all src/services/ for real AWS integration vs stubs. 4) Verify vector store providers actually connect to real backends. 5) Check benchmark_service for real metrics vs synthetic. 6) Review error handling, async patterns, config loading. 7) Judge code quality, dead code, unused imports. 8) Verify API routes actually call real services. Output: detailed findings report with file:line references for every issue found.
closeReason: Merged: backend review findings - 4 critical bugs, 3 high issues, 10 medium

title: Deep frontend code review: real API integration, no hardcoded data
description: Deep investigation of ALL React frontend code. Goals: 1) Identify ANY hardcoded mock data, fake responses, Math.random() values, placeholder text that should be real. 2) Verify all API calls use real endpoints (not mocked fetch). 3) Check Context providers wire to real API. 4) Review component quality - proper TypeScript types, error states, loading states. 5) Check deployment config page actually sends real Terraform commands. 6) Verify benchmark pages display real metrics. 7) Check for TODO comments indicating unfinished work. 8) Review package.json for unnecessary deps. Output: detailed findings report.
closeReason: Merged: frontend review report to docs/reviews/FRONTEND_REVIEW.md

title: Infrastructure review: Terraform deployment readiness for us-east-1, all vector DB variants
description: Deep review of Terraform infrastructure for real deployment to us-east-1. Goals: 1) Review ALL terraform/modules/ for deployment readiness - are they complete or stubs? 2) Analyze all vector DB deployment options on AWS with variants: OpenSearch (standard, with S3Vector backend, GPU-accelerated indexing), Qdrant (ECS, EBS-backed, EC2), LanceDB (ECS, EBS, EC2, S3, EFS backends), pgvector (Aurora PostgreSQL, RDS), Milvus (ECS, EKS), FAISS (Lambda, EC2, ECS), S3Vector (native). 3) Check Terraform profiles for completeness. 4) Verify IAM roles, security groups, VPC config. 5) Check cost estimation accuracy. 6) Identify missing modules needed for full deployment. 7) Verify us-east-1 region config throughout. Output: deployment readiness report with gaps list.
closeReason: Merged: infra deployment readiness report to terraform/DEPLOYMENT_READINESS_REPORT.md

title: Research: large multi-modal datasets for benchmarking (10K+ items)
description: Research and recommend large datasets for comprehensive vector DB benchmarking. Requirements: 1) Need 10,000+ items minimum per modality. 2) Modalities needed: TEXT (documents, paragraphs), IMAGE (photos, illustrations), AUDIO (speech, music, sound), VIDEO (clips, scenes). 3) Datasets must be freely available or on AWS Open Data Registry. 4) Consider: Common Crawl (text), LAION (images), AudioSet (audio), Kinetics/MSR-VTT (video), MS MARCO (text retrieval), Natural Questions, SQuAD. 5) For each dataset recommend: name, size, modality, download method, estimated storage, how to chunk for embedding. 6) Design ingestion strategy for each - how to batch process 10K+ items through Bedrock/SageMaker embedding models efficiently. 7) Consider AWS data transfer costs and S3 staging. 8) AWS-only focus - no third-party APIs for embedding. Output: dataset recommendations document with ingestion strategies.
closeReason: Merged: dataset research to docs/RESEARCH_DATASETS.md and docs/benchmarking/DATASET_RECOMMENDATIONS.md

title: Research: ALL AWS vector DB options with deployment variants and benchmark dimensions
description: Comprehensive research on every vector database option deployable on AWS with all variants. Cover: 1) S3Vector - native S3-based, serverless. 2) OpenSearch - standard, with S3Vector backend, with GPU-accelerated indexing (UltraWarm, ml nodes), serverless vs provisioned. 3) pgvector - Aurora PostgreSQL Serverless v2, RDS PostgreSQL, with pgvector extension, HNSW vs IVFFlat indexes. 4) Qdrant - on ECS Fargate, on EC2 with EBS (gp3/io2), on EKS. 5) LanceDB - on ECS with EBS, on EC2 with local NVMe, on S3 (native), on EFS for shared access. 6) Milvus - on EKS, on ECS, standalone vs distributed. 7) FAISS - on Lambda (for small), on EC2 with GPU, on SageMaker endpoints. 8) For each variant document: deployment complexity, cost model, scaling characteristics, max vector count, query latency expectations, persistence model, HA/DR options. 9) Recommend which variants to include in benchmark suite. Output: comprehensive comparison matrix.
closeReason: Merged: vectordb research to docs/reviews/VECTORDB_RESEARCH.md - 23 variants across 7 technologies

title: CRITICAL FIX: S3Vector and OpenSearch provider stubs → real implementations
description:
S3Vector and OpenSearch providers have stub upsert_vectors() and query() methods that ALWAYS return failure/empty. The unified ingestion service calls these and silently discards real embeddings.

FIX:
1. src/services/vector_store_s3vector_provider.py:216-264
   - upsert_vectors() must delegate to S3VectorStorageManager.put_vectors() with proper index ARN
   - query() must delegate to S3VectorStorageManager.query_vectors()
   - Read src/services/s3_vector_storage.py to understand the real API

2. src/services/vector_store_opensearch_provider.py:261-308
   - upsert_vectors() must use opensearch-py client to bulk index vectors
   - query() must use knn search API
   - Read src/services/opensearch/ directory for existing patterns

Both must conform to the VectorStoreProvider ABC interface. Test with real AWS calls pattern.
closeReason: Merged: S3Vector and OpenSearch providers now have real upsert/query implementations

title: CRITICAL FIX: Backend bugs — missing import, redundant API call, mock fallback, SQL injection, auth
description:
5 backend bugs to fix:

1. CRITICAL: src/services/bedrock_embedding.py:524 — missing 'import random'. Add import at top of file.

2. CRITICAL: src/services/bedrock_embedding.py:150-176 — generate_text_embedding() calls validate_model_access() which makes a REAL Bedrock API call with 'test' on every request. Remove the per-request validation or cache the result.

3. HIGH: src/ingestion/pipeline.py:57-64 — When INGESTION_STATE_MACHINE_ARN not set, silently returns mock success with job_id='mock-{uuid}'. Should raise an error or log a clear warning instead of pretending to succeed.

4. HIGH: src/services/vector_store_lancedb_provider.py:292-295 — SQL injection via filter_metadata. User-supplied keys/values directly interpolated into filter expression. Must sanitize/parameterize.

5. HIGH: src/api/middleware/auth.py:29-33 — When API_KEY not set, all auth disabled. Add explicit logging warning when running without auth. Consider requiring API_KEY in production (check for ENVIRONMENT=production env var).
closeReason: Merged: Fixed missing import, removed redundant API call, mock fallback raises error, SQL injection fixed, auth production guard

title: HIGH FIX: Frontend — localhost fallback, Math.random, console.log
description:
3 frontend issues:

1. HIGH: src/frontend/src/api/client.ts:4 and src/frontend/src/components/TerraformLogsViewer.tsx:42 — localhost:8000 fallback when VITE_API_URL not set. Should throw explicit error in production build or use window.location.origin as fallback.

2. HIGH: src/frontend/src/components/pages/InfrastructurePage.tsx — Math.random() values for metrics. Must use real API data or show 'N/A' / loading state when no data available.

3. MEDIUM: src/frontend/src/components/TerraformLogsViewer.tsx:45,52,56,63 — console.log statements. Remove or wrap in debug flag.

4. MEDIUM: window.confirm() in InfrastructurePage:123 for destructive actions — replace with a dialog component.
closeReason: Merged: Fixed localhost fallback, Math.random, console.log, window.confirm

title: HIGH FIX: Terraform security — IAM wildcards, EFS open, ALB HTTP, S3 encryption
description:
4 infrastructure security issues:

1. HIGH: IAM WILDCARD PERMISSIONS — 6 modules grant s3:* on Resource=*: videolake_backend_ecs, videolake_platform, benchmark_runner_ecs, benchmark_runner, lancedb_benchmark_ec2. Must scope to specific bucket ARNs using data.aws_caller_identity and data.aws_region.

2. HIGH: NFS OPEN TO INTERNET — lancedb_ecs/main.tf:75 EFS security group allows port 2049 from 0.0.0.0/0. Must restrict to VPC CIDR or private subnet CIDRs.

3. HIGH: ALB HTTP ONLY — videolake_backend_ecs has no HTTPS listener. Add ACM certificate resource and HTTPS listener on 443, redirect HTTP to HTTPS.

4. MEDIUM: S3 MISSING ENCRYPTION — lancedb_ecs S3 bucket and frontend_hosting bucket lack aws_s3_bucket_server_side_encryption_configuration. Add SSE-S3 or SSE-KMS.
closeReason: Merged: IAM scoped, EFS restricted, HTTPS added, S3 encryption added

title: MEDIUM FIX: Backend async patterns — blocking boto3, event loop, sleep
description: Medium backend issues from review: 1) src/services/benchmark_service.py:263 — blocking describe_tasks() in sync get_status(). 2) src/services/sagemaker_embedding_provider.py:360-365 — creates asyncio.new_event_loop() that can corrupt existing loop. 3) src/services/nova_embedding.py:569,575 — time.sleep(30) blocks thread. 4) src/ingestion/pipeline.py:34, nova_embedding.py:596 — direct boto3.client() bypassing factory. 5) vector_store_manager.py:127,157,185,250 — string literal 'FAILED' instead of enum. 6) benchmark_service.py:27 — in-memory job storage lost on restart. 7) benchmark_service.py:13-14 — sys.path manipulation.

title: Research+Update: Terraform AWS provider — first-class vector DB support
description: Check if AWS Terraform provider (currently ~>5.80) has added first-class support for: 1) S3 Tables / S3 Vectors (aws_s3tables_*, aws_s3vectors_*). 2) OpenSearch Serverless vector collections. 3) Aurora pgvector extension management. 4) Any new vector-related resources. Update provider version constraint to latest stable (5.90+?). Also check: random, archive, null providers for updates. Research using Terraform Registry and AWS provider changelog. Update all terraform required_providers blocks.
closeReason: Completed: Upgraded all 36 Terraform AWS provider constraints from ~>5.0/5.80 to ~>6.0 (latest stable 6.36.0). Gained access to native aws_s3vectors_* resources (added in 6.24.0). Also bumped random/null providers to latest minor versions. Applied terraform fmt. Created PROVIDER_UPGRADE_NOTES.md. Follow-up needed: update s3vector module to use native resources instead of null_resource workaround.

title: Migrate Python to uv — replace pip/requirements.txt
description: Migrate entire Python project to use uv package manager. Steps: 1) Create uv.lock from pyproject.toml. 2) Remove requirements.txt and requirements-benchmark.txt (replaced by pyproject.toml deps which uv reads natively). 3) Update all scripts that reference pip install to use uv. 4) Update Dockerfiles to use uv. 5) Update docs/QUICKSTART.md, README.md, docs/developer-guide.md with uv commands. 6) Add .python-version file if not exists. 7) Update CI/scripts to use: uv sync, uv run pytest, etc. 8) Keep pyproject.toml as the single source of truth for deps. 9) Update .gitignore for uv artifacts.
closeReason: Migrated Python project to uv package manager. Two builders completed: (1) uv-config-scripts: created .python-version, generated uv.lock, deleted requirements*.txt, updated scripts/verify_dev_setup.sh, scripts/deploy_platform.sh, start.sh, archive Dockerfiles, and docs. (2) uv-docs: updated QUICKSTART.md, README.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, docs/developer-guide.md, docs/DEVELOPMENT_SETUP.md with uv commands. Both branches sent as merge_ready.

title: Ensure bun for frontend — remove npm artifacts, update docs
description: Ensure bun is the sole JS package manager. Steps: 1) Remove src/frontend/package-lock.json (npm artifact — we use bun.lock). 2) Verify all scripts use bun commands (bun install, bun run dev, bun run build). 3) Update any docs referencing npm to use bun instead. 4) Check Dockerfiles for npm references. 5) Verify bun.lock is up to date.
closeReason: Made bun the sole JS package manager. Deleted package-lock.json, updated start.sh to require bun (no npm fallback), updated all deploy scripts and documentation to use bun exclusively. 14 files modified across scripts and docs. Archive files intentionally untouched.

title: Update all deps to latest compatible versions — Python (boto3, fastapi, etc) + frontend
description: Update all dependencies to latest compatible versions. Python (in pyproject.toml): 1) boto3/botocore to latest (1.36+?). 2) fastapi to latest (0.115+?). 3) pydantic to latest. 4) lancedb, qdrant-client, opensearch-py to latest. 5) All other deps. Check changelogs for breaking changes. Frontend (in package.json): 1) React 19 — check if latest patch. 2) All @radix-ui deps. 3) tailwindcss, vite, typescript. 4) tanstack/react-query. Run uv lock after Python updates. Run bun install after frontend updates. Verify nothing breaks.
closeReason: Python dependencies updated to latest compatible versions. boto3 1.35→1.42, fastapi 0.115→0.135, pydantic 2.10→2.12, lancedb 0.18→0.29, qdrant-client 1.12→1.17, opensearch-py 2.8→3.1, numpy 1.26→2.2, scikit-learn 1.6→1.7, pandas 2.2→2.3, plus dev deps. uv.lock regenerated. Frontend updates split to separate task.

title: Merge remaining tooling branches: py-deps, fe-deps, uv-docs
description:
Three branches need merging but have uv.lock/bun.lock conflicts that require regeneration:

1. overstory/py-deps-builder/S3Vector-3270 — bumps Python deps in pyproject.toml (boto3 1.42, fastapi 0.135, lancedb 0.29, etc). uv.lock conflicts with master.
2. overstory/fe-deps-builder/S3Vector-3270 — bumps frontend deps in src/frontend/package.json (React 19.2, vite 8, vitest 4). bun.lock conflicts expected.
3. overstory/uv-docs/S3Vector-2fae — doc updates, most already ported by merge-builder. Check if any unique content remains.

Approach: Cherry-pick the pyproject.toml and package.json changes onto a fresh branch from master, run uv lock and cd src/frontend && bun install to regenerate lockfiles, commit, and signal merge_ready.

Also add pymilvus and faiss-cpu to pyproject.toml dependencies (they were missed in the original deps-update).
closeReason: Merged dependency bumps from py-deps and fe-deps branches. Python deps bumped (boto3 1.42, fastapi 0.135, pydantic 2.12, numpy 2.2, lancedb 0.29, etc). Added pymilvus>=2.6.0 and faiss-cpu>=1.10.0. Frontend deps bumped (react 19.2, vite 7.3, eslint 9.39, etc). Lockfiles regenerated. uv-docs branch confirmed fully ported already — skipped.
